prerequisites: A Nebius account, created at the Nebius Token Factory console (https://tokenfactory.nebius.com) using a Google or GitHub login.
An API key generated from the Nebius Token Factory console, to supply as the required api_key field.
A start_date value to bound the data the connector reads (required).
Optionally, a limit value to cap the number of records returned per request (optional).

authMethods.name: API Key (Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate with a single Nebius API key, mapped to the connector's api_key secret field. Nebius exposes an OpenAI-compatible API at https://api.tokenfactory.nebius.com/v1/ and passes the key as an HTTP Authorization: Bearer <api_key> header. Generate the key in the Nebius Token Factory console and keep it private; do not expose it in client-side code.

setupSteps.title: Create a Nebius account
setupSteps.body: Go to https://tokenfactory.nebius.com and create an account. Log in using your Google or GitHub account.

setupSteps.title: Generate an API key
setupSteps.body: In the Nebius Token Factory console, open the API keys page (https://tokenfactory.nebius.com/project/api-keys) and create a new API key. Copy the key value immediately and store it securely; do not expose it in client-side code.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the generated key into the api_key field. Set start_date to the date from which the connector should begin reading data. Optionally set limit to cap the number of records returned per request.

sources.title: Introduction (Authentication) - Nebius Token Factory API reference
API keys - Nebius Token Factory console
